name: Gnocchi Allo Safferano
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Tomatoes (chopped fine in a food processor)
recipeInstructions:
1. Fat grams per serving: Approx.
2. Cook Time: 1:30
To make gnocchi:
1. Pour flour onto a work surface and make a well in the center.
2. Pour olive oil into the well.
3. Dissolve saffron in a small amount of warm water and add to the well.
4. Mix the liquid with the flour, adding enough water to form a firm, not sticky dough. Add water 1/4 cup at a time until the dough reaches the desired consistency.
5. Cut the dough into pieces and roll into long cylinders about 1/2 inch in diameter.
6. Cut the cylinders into 1-inch chunks and press your finger into each gnocchi to make a deep dent. Set aside on a floured surface to dry while making the sauce.
To prepare the sauce:
1. Heat pork fat in a sauté pan and cook onion and parsley until tender.
2. Stir in ground meat and cook until no longer pink.
3. Add chopped tomatoes, bay leaves, salt, and pepper.
4. Cook, uncovered, for an hour or until the sauce thickens.
5. Cook gnocchi in boiling salted water until cooked al dente. Drain and arrange in pasta dishes.
6. Pour sauce over top and garnish with a handful of grated cheese.
7. Serves 4-6.
